cross_findings.finding: The strongest repeated pattern is product and engineering teams needing one system for scaled planning, issue tracking, dependencies, and customer feedback intake.

evidence.claim: Linear groups product work across issues, projects, initiatives, cycles, customer requests, views, analytics, and integrations.
OpenAI adopted Linear across thousands of users as teams scaled and needed alignment across complex dependencies.
Linear customer requests connect feedback from support tools, CRM tools, email, and Slack to product work.

cross_findings.finding: Enterprise and business-package evaluation appears as a readiness risk because admin controls and onboarding requirements show up in offer and VoC evidence.

evidence.claim: Linear Business and Enterprise plans include package differences around private teams, guests, admin controls, SAML, SCIM, and onboarding support.
Enterprise evaluation may involve SAML, SCIM, granular admin controls, invoice or PO billing, and onboarding support.
Pricing-stage demand should account for package comparison across Free, Basic, Business, and Enterprise plans.

contradictions.conflict: The market and identity evidence frames Linear as a broad product development system, while keyword evidence narrows visible demand to issue tracking, roadmap, customer requests, and integrations.

sides.claim: Linear describes itself as a product development system for teams and agents to plan, build, and monitor product work.
The category demand pattern centers on product development system, issue tracking, roadmap, customer requests, and integrations.

contradictions.resolution_needed: Downstream positioning must decide whether to lead with the broad product development system category or more specific workflow-intent terms.

research_gaps.gap: Competitor evidence names current alternatives but does not provide sourced comparison details for why each alternative loses or wins against Linear.
research_gaps.blocked_downstream_decision: Positioning cannot choose a comparison angle for Jira, GitHub Issues, or Asana without sourced competitor-difference evidence.

high_confidence_themes.finding: Scaled team coordination is supported by identity, ICP, competitor, and VoC evidence.

evidence.claim: Linear describes itself as a product development system for teams and agents to plan, build, and monitor product work.
OpenAI adopted Linear across thousands of users as teams scaled and needed alignment across complex dependencies.
OpenAI's story describes cross-team handoffs, stale statuses, and disconnected systems as collaboration problems.

readiness_blockers.gap: Sourced competitor-difference evidence is thin for the named alternatives in the competitor set.
readiness_blockers.blocked_downstream_decision: Downstream positioning and scripts should not claim superiority over Jira, GitHub Issues, or Asana until the competitor card includes sourced comparison evidence.
